title: Add roadmap / epic-chaining authoring guidance to SKILL.md
description:
Implement spec §1 (docs/design/execution-model-v2-epic-chaining-and-learning-loop.md) in skills/ticks/SKILL.md.

Add a 'Roadmaps (multi-epic work)' section to the tick-creation guidance:
- A roadmap is a chain of epics linked with existing --blocked-by; only the front (unblocked) epic gets child ticks; downstream epics are parent-only with rough-scope descriptions (paragraph + deliverables list).
- When fleshing out the front epic, ALWAYS append a final child task: 'Close out <epic>: run epic retro, then flesh out <next epic> into ticks' — real work, not a handoff marker.
- Human gates are chosen at roadmap creation: create a downstream epic with --awaiting checkpoint or --requires approval to force a stop at that boundary; default is auto-continue.
- Roadmap-level changes (add/remove/reorder epics) are human decisions; the agent may propose them in the retro report only.
- Subsume the existing 'For phased specs: create ticks for the current/next phase only' note into this section (it is the same rule).

Files: skills/ticks/SKILL.md only. Match existing tone/format (imperative guidance + small bash examples).
acceptance_criteria: SKILL.md documents roadmap chaining incl. the close-out-task convention and gate flags; old phased-spec note merged in, not duplicated; no contradiction with references/claude-runner.md
